title: Gentianella cruciata
taxonomy: Gentianella cruciata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Gentianella cruciata is a member of the genus Gentianella, which consists of several species of gentians. The plant is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found in moist environments. Gentianella cruciata is a relatively rare species within its genus, and its classification helps in understanding its unique characteristics and distribution.

intro: Gentianella cruciata, also known as Cross-leaved Gentian, is a low-growing, perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family. Traditionally, Gentianella cruciata has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and gentianine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gentianella cruciata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and decoctions to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
